info.name: TicketFlow API
info.description:
Production Postman collection for the TicketFlow mock backend (Express 5 + better-sqlite3). Every endpoint is covered with complete request/response examples and automated schema tests.

**Run POST /auth/login first** — the test script stores the token in the `token` collection variable automatically.

## Seed users
| Email | Password |
|---|---|
| sofia.hernandez@ticketflow.com | ticket123 |
| mateo.garcia@ticketflow.com | ticket123 |
| valeria.lopez@ticketflow.com | ticket123 |
| diego.martinez@ticketflow.com | ticket123 |

## Typical flow
1. POST /auth/login → token stored
2. POST /payment/process → transactionId stored
3. POST /bookings → bookingId stored
4. GET /bookings → list user bookings
5. PATCH /bookings/:id/cancel → cancel a booking
6. POST /auth/logout → invalidate token

## Auth
All endpoints except `GET /health` and `POST /auth/login` require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

## Error shape (all errors)
`{ "error": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human readable description" }`

## Critical field distinctions
- `seat.zone` in `GET /events/:id/seats` is a **zone ID** string (e.g. `zon-001`) that references `zones[].id`
- `booking.zone` in `POST /bookings` and `GET /bookings` is the **zone name** string (e.g. `VIP`)
- `cancelledAt` in `PATCH /bookings/:id/cancel` response is ISO 8601 from Node.js `new Date().toISOString()` (e.g. `2026-07-04T16:00:00.000Z`)
- `cancelledAt` in `GET /bookings` response is SQLite `datetime('now')` format (e.g. `2026-07-04 16:00:00`) or `null`

## Database
- In-memory SQLite — resets to seed state on every server restart
- 20 events across 3 venues, 188 seats, 4 users, 8 seed bookings

variable.description: Base URL of the TicketFlow API
Bearer token — populated automatically by POST /auth/login test script
Booking ID — populated automatically by POST /bookings test script
Payment transaction ID — populated automatically by POST /payment/process test script

item.name: Health
item.description: Public health check — no authentication required.

request.description: Public liveness probe. Returns 200 when the server is running. No authentication required.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Login and logout. Login is public. Logout requires a valid Bearer token.

exec: pm.test("Response has token with correct format", function () {
pm.test("Response has user with all required fields", function () {

request.description:
Authenticate with email and password. Returns an opaque Bearer token and the user object. The test script stores the token in `{{token}}` automatically.

**Required body fields:** `email` (string), `password` (string)

exec: pm.test("Clear token variable after logout", function () {

request.description:
Invalidates the current Bearer token. After this call, any request using the same token will receive 401. The test script clears `{{token}}` automatically.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

exec: pm.test("User has all required fields with correct types", function () {

request.description:
Returns the profile of the authenticated user. Identity is derived from the Bearer token — no user ID parameter is needed.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

item.name: Events
item.description: Event listing and seat map. All protected.

pm.test("Each event has all required fields with correct types", function () {
pm.test("Events are ordered by date ASC", function () {

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of events ordered by `date ASC`. Same fields as `GET /events`, just paginated — does not replace or alter that endpoint.

**Query parameters (all optional):**
- `page` — page number, default 1
- `limit` — items per page, default 10, max 50 (server-clamped)

**Seed data:** 20 events across 3 venues (ven-001 / ven-002 / ven-003)

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 200 OK — Page 1, limit 10 (10 of 20 events)

exec: pm.test("Response has all required top-level fields", function () {
pm.test("Each zone has all required fields", function () {
pm.test("Zones are ordered by price DESC", function () {
pm.test("Each seat has all required fields — seat.zone is zone ID not zone name", function () {
pm.test("Seats are ordered by row ASC then col ASC", function () {

variable.description: Event ID. Seats belong to the event's venue, shared across all events at the same venue.

request.description:
Returns the venue type, price zones, and full seat map for the given event.

**IMPORTANT:** `seat.zone` is the **zone ID** (e.g. `zon-001`), not the zone name. To resolve zone details, cross-reference with the `zones` array using `zones[].id`.

**Venue types:** `arena` | `halfmoon` | `flat`
**Seat statuses:** `available` | `occupied`
**Zone ordering:** zones sorted by price DESC
**Seat ordering:** seats sorted by row ASC, col ASC

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 200 OK — Seat map for evt-001 (ven-001, arena, 3 zones)
404 Not Found — Event does not exist

item.name: Payment
item.description: Payment processing simulation. Protected. Has a 10% random failure rate.

exec: pm.test("Approved: all required fields present with correct types", function () {
pm.test("Declined: error response has correct shape", function () {

request.description:
Simulates payment processing. Introduces an artificial 2–5 second delay to mimic real payment gateway latency.

**10% random failure rate** — one in ten calls returns 402 PAYMENT_DECLINED regardless of input. This is by design.

**Required body fields:**
- `method` (string, enum): `card` | `paypal`

On success, the test script stores `transactionId` in `{{transactionId}}` for use in POST /bookings.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 200 OK — Payment approved
response.body: {"transactionId":"txn-583921","status":"approved","message":"Payment approved. You will receive a confirmation email.","processedAt":"2026-07-04T15:30:00.000Z"}

response.name: 402 Payment Required — Payment declined (10% random)
response.body: {"error":"PAYMENT_DECLINED","message":"Your payment was declined. Please try again."}

item.name: Bookings
item.description: Booking management. All endpoints protected. GET /bookings only returns the authenticated user's own bookings.

exec: pm.test("Booking has all required fields with correct types and values", function () {

request.description:
Creates a confirmed booking. Atomically marks the seat as `occupied`.

**Required body fields:**
- `eventId` (string) — event ID
- `seatId` (string) — seat ID; must have `status: available`
- `contactEmail` (string)
- `contactPhone` (string)
- `payment` (object) — `{ method: "card"|"paypal", transactionId: string }`

**Optional body fields:**
- `total` (number) — defaults to `0` if omitted

**Business rules:**
- Booking ID format: `TF-` + 6 random digits
- Returns 409 if the seat is already `occupied`
- `cancelledAt` is always `null` on creation
- `booking.zone` is the zone **name** (e.g. `VIP`), not the zone ID

The test script stores the new `bookingId` in `{{bookingId}}` automatically.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 400 Bad Request — Missing required fields
response.body: {"error":"VALIDATION_ERROR","message":"eventId, seatId, contactEmail, contactPhone and payment are required"}

exec: pm.test("Response has data array and pagination object", function () {
pm.test("Each booking item has all required fields with correct types", function () {

query.description: Filter by booking status. Enum: confirmed | pending | cancelled.
Partial match on event name. Case-insensitive SQL LIKE (%value%).
Filter events on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).
Filter events on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of the authenticated user's bookings, ordered by `createdAt DESC`.

**Scope:** Only returns bookings belonging to the authenticated user. No cross-user access.

**Query parameters (all optional):**
- `page` — page number, default 1
- `limit` — items per page, default 10, max 50
- `status` — filter: `confirmed` | `pending` | `cancelled`
- `eventName` — partial match (SQL LIKE `%value%`)
- `dateFrom` — filter events on or after date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `dateTo` — filter events on or before date (YYYY-MM-DD)

**Field note:** `cancelledAt` is SQLite datetime format (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) or `null`. This differs from the ISO 8601 format returned by `PATCH /bookings/:id/cancel`.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 200 OK — All bookings, no filters (user: sofia / usr-001)
200 OK — Cancelled booking example (cancelledAt is SQLite datetime string)
200 OK — Empty result (no bookings match filter)

exec: pm.test("Cancel response has all required fields", function () {

variable.description: Booking ID in format TF-XXXXXX. Must belong to the authenticated user.

request.description:
Cancels a booking and releases the seat back to `available`.

**Business rules:**
- The booking must belong to the authenticated user
- Only non-cancelled bookings can be cancelled (idempotency is NOT supported — calling this twice returns 409)
- Releases the seat to `available` so other users can book it

**Response field note:** `cancelledAt` is ISO 8601 from `new Date().toISOString()` (e.g. `2026-07-04T16:00:00.000Z`). This differs from the SQLite datetime string format (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) returned by `GET /bookings`.

**Protected:** Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 404 Not Found — Booking does not exist or belongs to another user
409 Conflict — Booking already cancelled
response.body: {"error":"INVALID_TRANSITION","message":"This booking cannot be cancelled in its current state"}
